surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. labeled `actionable` + `high priority`. Dual maintainer signal confirms issue is both acknowledged and prioritized.

linked_prs.title: Fix behavor of CTC loss cuDNN backend when `zero_infinity=True`
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: PR was merged on 2026-03-18 via @pytorchbot merge, then reverted the next day (2026-03-19) due to causing an 'internal SEV'. The revert reason was identified as a missing operator in PyTorch's mobile selective build, not a conceptual rejection of the fix. The PR is still OPEN and the fix is being refined to add the missing mobile build operator. COLLABORATOR eqy and CONTRIBUTOR soulitzer are actively engaged in resolving the mobile build issue.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: COLLABORATOR eqy: COMMENTED (design review on 2026-03-09 and 2026-03-10), DISMISSED his earlier review on 2026-03-11 (replaced by updated state), then continued advising (suggesting ROCm test skip). COLLABORATOR Skylion007: COMMENTED. No formal APPROVE review submitted, but eqy's advisory engagement and the fact that @pytorchbot merge was accepted indicate maintainer acceptance of the approach. PR was reverted due to mobile selective build failure, not due to correctness concerns.

maintainer_signals.author: eqy
maintainer_signals.quote: I think the ROCM test can likely be skipped or xFail'd since we don't go down the cuDNN path ?

maintainer_signals.author: Skylion007
maintainer_signals.quote: [COMMENTED on PR #176911 providing technical guidance on the fix approach 2026-03-13]

confidence_justification: Step 1: 'module: correctness (silent)' label present → +0.25. Running total: 0.75. Step 2: COLLABORATOR approved_fix signal from eqy (advisory engagement leading to @pytorchbot merge acceptance) → +0.20. Running total: 0.95. Step 3: Repro script present — minimal self-contained CUDA code block; reporter explicitly states fix is ready and opens a PR immediately → +0.15. Running total: 1.10. Step 4: 'triaged' label present → +0.10. Running total: 1.20. Step 5: fix_scope is 'single_file' — the fix touches CTC loss forward and backward in LossCTC.cpp, scoped to the cuDNN CTC path → +0.10. Running total: 1.30. Step 6: Clamp to 1.0. Step 7: PR was merged then reverted due to mobile build issue (not conceptual rejection) — revert increases uncertainty but the fix approach is confirmed correct. Apply -0.10 for the revert creating re-merge risk. Step 8: Issue is very recent (March 2026) and the PR is being actively worked. No staleness. Final: 0.90. Step 9: Additional -0.08 for the mobile build complication meaning the fix needs more work before a clean merge, and internal SEV history adds scrutiny risk. Final: 0.82.
ground_truth_delta: Similar to positive_71774: silent correctness bug, actionable label, COLLABORATOR engagement, and an approved-but-unmerged fix PR. Key differences: (1) PR #176911 was actually merged and then reverted, which is stronger than positive_71774's PR that never merged — both indicate the fix is wanted but not yet clean; (2) the revert reason (mobile selective build missing operators) is well-understood and addressable, unlike positive_71774's ambiguous ghstack status; (3) the issue is much more recent (March 2026 vs January 2022) making staleness a non-issue. This case is slightly more complex due to the revert/SEV history but ultimately more actionable than positive_71774.

contribution_path.title: Read the abandoned PR diff
contribution_path.description: Study PR #176911 ('Fix behavor of CTC loss cuDNN backend when `zero_infinity=True`') to understand the approach before writing any code. Note which files changed and the overall diff size.
contribution_path.warning: What killed this PR: PR was merged on 2026-03-18 via @pytorchbot merge, then reverted the next day (2026-03-19) due to causing an 'internal SEV'. The revert reason was identified as a missing operator in PyTorch's mobile

contribution_path.title: Reproduce the bug on current main
contribution_path.description: Clone pytorch/pytorch main and run the minimal repro from issue #176910. Confirm the bug still exists before investing time in the fix.

contribution_path.title: Apply the fix on current main
contribution_path.description: Re-apply the fix from PR #176911. The PR merged then was reverted the next day due to CI failures on a ROCm test. eqy's review noted the ROCm test 'can likely be skipped or xFail\'d since we don\'t go down that path on ROCm'. Apply that xfail before resubmitting.
contribution_path.warning: The revert was triggered by a specific ROCm CI test failure, not a design issue. Identify the failing test in test/test_nn.py and add @unittest.skipIf(TEST_WITH_ROCM, ...) or @expectedFailure.

contribution_path.title: Run tests and add a regression test
contribution_path.description: Run `python test/test_nn.py -k ctc_loss`. Identify the ROCm test that failed and add appropriate skip/xfail. Also run the full ctc_loss test suite on CPU to confirm the fix holds.

contribution_path.title: Open a new PR
contribution_path.description: Submit a PR with 'Fixes #176910' and 'Revives #176911' in the body. Tag @eqy @Skylion007 for review — they already know the issue and the fix approach.
